summary: Re-ran full backend test suite after main agent applied fixes for daily-pdf route ordering and brute-force identifier. ALL 30/30 tests PASSED (100%). Both critical bugs from iteration_1 are confirmed FIXED.

minor.issue: Still does not re-sync admin password from env on subsequent runs (only role/permissions/active are reset). Documented behavior; keep in mind for production resets.
No de-duplication / upsert on import — repeated imports create duplicates. Acceptable for MVP.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is still ~1100+ lines; consider splitting into modules (auth.py, clients.py, products.py, orders.py, dashboard.py, pdf.py) for maintainability.
Cookie `secure=False` hardcoded; for production HTTPS it should be env-driven.
Optional: add upsert-by-unique-field for CSV imports to avoid duplicates on re-import.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed records and tear them down via fixtures; no leftover data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green. Both critical fixes verified: (1) GET /api/orders/daily-pdf returns application/pdf 200 (route now declared before /orders/{order_id}); (2) Brute force triggers 429 after 5 failed attempts (identifier changed to email-only). Re-run with: cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml

rca of the issue.daily_pdf_404: RESOLVED — route /orders/daily-pdf now declared before /orders/{order_id}. test_daily_pdf passes (Content-Type: application/pdf, 200).
rca of the issue.brute_force_not_triggering: RESOLVED — identifier changed from 'ip:email' to 'email:{email}'. test_brute_force_protection passes (6th attempt returns 429).
